1: A direct: "Behold, how good and pleasant it is when brothers dwell in unity!" John 17:21-23 — "that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me, and I in you, that they also may be in us." Christ prays for the unity the psalm describes — and he creates it through his death. Ephesians 2:14-16 — "he himself is our peace, who has made the two groups one and has destroyed the barrier, the dividing wall of hostility... creating in himself one new humanity." The psalm's vision is Christ's own intention.
2: A type: "It is like the precious oil on the head, running down on the beard, on the beard of Aaron, running down on the collar of his robes." The anointing oil flowing from Aaron's head is a type of the Spirit poured out from Christ at Pentecost. Acts 2:33 — "Being therefore exalted at the right hand of God, and having received from the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this that you yourselves are seeing and hearing." Christ is the Anointed One (Māšîaḥ = Christos) from whom the anointing flows to all his people (1 John 2:20,27 — "you have been anointed by the Holy One").
3: A direct: "For there the LORD has commanded the blessing, life forevermore." John 17:3 — "And this is eternal life, that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent." Revelation 22:14 — "Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that they may have the right to the tree of life." The "life forevermore" commanded as a blessing at Zion is the eternal life that Christ gives to all who are united in him. The blessing, the unity, and the life all converge in Christ.
